name: vibe3d
description: simple 3d modeller inspired MODO and LigthWave
_comment-onnx-per-config: d-onnxruntime (AI candidate ranker backend) is NOT a base dependency — it is added per-configuration alongside the `WithAI` version so the `modeling-noai` build can omit it entirely. onnxruntime.dll statically imports api-ms-win-core-path (Windows 8+), so dropping it is what makes a Windows 7-capable build possible. Only source/ai/onnx_backend.d touches the binding; all of it is gated by version(WithAI).
_comment-assimp-static: assimp is static-linked via subConfigurations.bindbc-assimp6=static. ALL build+link config (per-platform preBuildCommands that build the minimal OBJ/glTF/FBX libassimp, lflags by explicit archive path, and stdc++/m) now lives in the bindbc-assimp6 `static` configuration — a clean clone auto-builds it, no manual prerequisite. The base BindAssimp_Static version below is for vibe3d's own source/io/* guards.

profile-fp._comment: Profiling build: forces frame pointers so perf --call-graph fp produces clean stacks without DWARF unwind cost. Use: `dub build --build=profile-fp` then `perf record -F 999 --call-graph fp -p $(pgrep vibe3d) sleep 5`. DMD uses `-gs`; LDC uses `--frame-pointer=all` (its `-gs` equivalent) — kept separate so the buildType works under either compiler.

perf._comment: Perf-harness build: optimized + inlined release with PerfProbe instrumentation active. releaseMode strips bounds-checks/asserts off the hot per-vertex loops so the timers measure the tool, not bounds-checking. The default build defines no PerfProbe version, so g_perf calls compile to no-ops. Use: `dub build --build=perf --compiler=ldc2`.

perf-count._comment: Debug build with PerfProbe active but no optimizer, for CI counter invariants: counts are control-flow, so ldc/optimize/stable hardware are unnecessary. dmd-buildable. Default build defines no PerfProbe, so this never affects it.

configurations.name: modeling
configurations._comment: Default. Modeling-only build — no IPR, no Cycles/RPR runtime, no D-Cycles/D-RadeonProRender dependencies. Fast compile, no extra system libs needed. All code under source/render/* and source/material/* is excluded from the build (gated by `version (WithRender)`). Includes the AI candidate ranker (version WithAI + d-onnxruntime).

configurations.name: modeling-noai
configurations._comment: AI-less modeling build (Windows 7 target). Same as `modeling` but WithAI is undefined and d-onnxruntime is NOT pulled in, so source/ai/onnx_backend.d compiles to an empty module and the statusline AI button renders as a disabled placeholder. The pure-D AI heuristics still compile but are unreachable (master switch stays off). Rationale: onnxruntime.dll statically imports api-ms-win-core-path-l1-1-0.dll (Windows 8+) — omitting it is the prerequisite for a binary that can load on Windows 7. CI's Win7 zip pairs this config with app-local UCRT + VC++ runtime DLLs.

configurations.name: with-render
configurations._comment: Opt-in build with two render backends (Cycles via D-Cycles, RPR via D-RadeonProRender) plus the AI ranker. The default `modeling` config never references the render repos so `dub build` works on machines that have neither. Git dependencies are pinned to commit SHAs so CI can reuse the persistent dub cache instead of re-cloning heavyweight repositories. For active backend development, register a local override once: `dub add-local ~/Code/D-RadeonProRender && dub add-local ~/Code/D-Cycles`. D-Cycles builds its C shim via CMake (Blender LFS submodule + optional CUDA/OptiX); D-RadeonProRender is dynamic-loaded bindbc. See doc/shader_framework_plan.md.
